description:
## The change

Now that the IR distinguishes `[]` from `nil` (EL.1), give them
distinct runtime bit patterns. This is the foundational behavior
change of the epic.

### Add

  - `EMPTY_LIST_BITS = 0x8` in `runtime/src/fz_value.rs` (TAG_PTR space,
    unmapped low-address sentinel â€” no malloc ever returns 0x8).
  - `FzValue::EMPTY_LIST` constant.
  - `FzValue::is_empty_list(self) -> bool` predicate.
  - `EMPTY_LIST_BITS` constant in `src/ir_codegen.rs`.

### Change

  - `Const::EmptyList` codegen â†’ `iconst EMPTY_LIST_BITS` (was NIL_BITS).
  - `Prim::IsEmptyList` codegen â†’ compare to `EMPTY_LIST_BITS` (was NIL_BITS).
  - `Prim::MakeList(vec![], None)` codegen â†’ `EMPTY_LIST_BITS` (was NIL_BITS).
  - `ir_interp` parallel: equality + empty-list checks against new bits.
  - Pretty-print: print `[]` for `EMPTY_LIST` (distinct from `nil`).
  - Equality fold: `Descr::list_of(none()).is_equiv(Descr::nil())` â€”
    audit and fix if it returned true under conflation.

### REMOVES

  - `NIL_BITS` as the canonical empty-list representation in codegen +
    runtime + interp paths.
  - Any silent assumption that walking a cons list terminates at
    `is_nil` (now must be `is_empty_list`).

## Expected effect

  - After this commit, `nil != []` at the value level. A function
    expecting a list rejects `nil` at @spec validation time. A
    function pattern-matching on `nil` doesn't match `[]`. Etc.
  - CLIF goldens shift (different iconst values). Re-bless.
  - `expected.txt` unchanged everywhere â€” every fixture's observable
    runtime output preserved.
  - All 842 tests pass.

## Depends on

EL.1.

## Walkthrough hazards

  - **0x8 as sentinel**: must verify no `alloc_cons` (or any
    allocator) ever returns 0x8. On modern OSes the entire first
    page (addresses 0â€“4095) is unmapped â€” any attempt to access
    triggers a SEGV. Sentinel value is safe.
  - **List traversal**: every cons-walking loop must check
    `is_empty_list(cur)` before dereferencing. The rename in EL.1
    threaded `IsEmptyList` everywhere; this ticket just changes what
    bits that check compares against. Grep for any DIRECT
    `== NIL_BITS` for list-end (likely none after EL.1).
  - **print() for list values**: `print([1, 2, 3])` walks cons cells,
    stops when `cur == EMPTY_LIST_BITS`. Update from the previous
    `cur == NIL_BITS` termination. Distinct from `print(nil)` which
    sees `cur == NIL_BITS` and prints "nil".
  - **fz_runtime stub for `nil` halt value**: at scheduler-halt, the
    halt value may be inspected; ensure `is_nil()` and `is_empty_list()`
    are not confused.
title: fz-EL.2: runtime bit-pattern split â€” EMPTY_LIST sentinel
